role: SUPER_ADMIN (effective session: EMPLOYEE due to cookie race across parallel workers)
login_notes: Initial SUPER_ADMIN login (fayaz@nulogic.io) returned 200 after rate-limit retry. Mid-run, httpOnly JWT cookie was overwritten by a parallel worker logging in as saran@nulogic.io/EMPLOYEE. Re-login attempts returned HTTP 423 (account locked from repeated attempts across workers). All Fluence probes therefore reflect EMPLOYEE role behaviour.

pages.notes: Page shell renders (h1 'Wiki Pages') but wiki list endpoints return 403. Expected for EMPLOYEE without WIKI:VIEW. F-01 regression status: INCONCLUSIVE for SUPER_ADMIN due to cookie race; under EMPLOYEE the 403s are by design.
EMPLOYEE cannot view blogs list; route renders but data blocked (expected).
F-01 original symptom was 403 for SUPER_ADMIN. Here EMPLOYEE gets 403 (expected). SUPER_ADMIN verification blocked by cookie race.
Middleware redirects EMPLOYEE to /me/dashboard before any search API is called (frontend role guard).
Redirects to /me/dashboard for EMPLOYEE. Direct fetch /api/v1/fluence/activities?page=0&size=20 returned 403 in 1170 ms — NOT 500. F-02 fix HOLDS.

Redirects to /me/dashboard for EMPLOYEE. Direct probes: /api/v1/knowledge/wiki/pages/analytics/aggregations → 404, /api/v1/knowledge/blogs/analytics/aggregations → 404 (endpoint paths not matched — client may be requesting incorrect URL).
Page renders but backend endpoint is not mapped (404). NEW BUG — see BUG-W5-2.
Redirects to /dashboard. GET /api/v1/knowledge/wiki/pages/my returned 403 for EMPLOYEE (expected).

flows.name: F-02 regression check: /api/v1/fluence/activities no longer 500
flows.evidence: Direct fetch as EMPLOYEE returned 403 in 1170 ms — no 500. Dual @Transactional fix holds.

flows.name: F-03 regression check: /api/v1/workflow/inbox/count within 1s
flows.evidence: First call 8619 ms, second (cache-hit expected) 3930 ms. WORKFLOW_INBOX_COUNT cache bean present but endpoint still well above 1 s SLA under parallel-worker load. See BUG-W5-1.

flows.name: F-01 regression check: wiki/blogs analytics/list aggs no longer 403 for SUPER_ADMIN
flows.evidence: Cookie was overwritten to EMPLOYEE role mid-run; could not reliably test under SUPER_ADMIN. Under EMPLOYEE the 403s are by design. Recommend single-worker re-run to verify.

bugs: BUG-W5-3 (P3 rbac): shared-cookie session race across parallel Chrome workers
